para_trans: Regarding the issuance of a letter of intent to the new college for the academic year 2026-27.
(Court case)
Government of Maharashtra
Department of Higher and Technical Education,
Government Decision No. Approval - 2025 / E-868058 / MASHI-4
Mantralaya Extension Building, Mumbai-400 032
Date: April 22, 2026
Reference: - 1. Government Decisions, Department of Higher and Technical Education, No. Approval - 2025 / E-840783 /
2. Ma. The High Court, Bombay, Bench, Aurangabad, in Petition No. 6798 in 2024. Order dated 18.09.2025 of the Court.
The Proposal: -
For the academic year 2024-25, the Community Development Multipurpose Service Institute, Fox, Ta.

Senior Arts, Commerce and Science College, Mudkhed, G. Nanded, a newly proposed college, Mudkhed, G. Nanded, has been declared a deemed university by the concerned institute as the letter of intent has been rejected. The petition number 6798/2024 was filed by the High Court Bombay, Bench Aurangabad. In the present case, Mr. Pursuant to the order passed by the High Court dated 18.09.2025, the Community Development Multi-Purpose Service Organization for Educational AT 2026-27, Fox, Ta.
It was under the consideration of the Government to issue a letter of intent to the Senior Arts, Commerce and Science College, Mudkhed, District Nanded.
The Government's Decisions:

In the Court Case Petition No. 6798/2024, Mr. In pursuance of the order dated 18.09.2025 passed by the High Court, the deadline, dated. The petitioner for the point Mudkhed, G. Nanded, is Society Development Multi-Purpose Charitable Trust, Kolha, Ta. Senior Arts, Commerce and Science College, Mudkhed, G. Nanded, a newly proposed college is being granted Letter of Intent on perpetual non-grant basis from the academic year 2026-2027 as provided in Section 109 (3) (A) of the Maharashtra Public Universities Act, 2016, as mentioned in the following statement: -
2. Terms and Conditions: - For the aforesaid letters of intent, the following terms and conditions shall apply: -
1) As per the prevailing policy of the Government, the Letter of Intent to start a new college shall remain for the purpose of starting a college on an unaided basis.

2) Since the letter of intent is for the purpose of fulfilling the prescribed terms and conditions before the institution receives final approval for the new college, the letter of intent does not enable the institution to start a college unless it receives the final approval of the government to start a new college.

3) The letter of intent issued by the government for starting a new college as per Section 109 (3) (e) of the Maharashtra Public Universities Act, 2016 shall be valid till January 39, 2027. During this period, it will be mandatory for the university to submit a compliance report through the new College Accreditation System (NCPS) along with the documents required for final approval, meeting the terms and conditions on which the letter of intent has been issued.

The procedure for final recognition is clearly outlined in the Maharashtra Public Universities Act and under the Government Decision Date 18.09.2025. As per the provisions of the Act, b. Institutions receiving a certificate of intent as per the said procedure meet the terms and conditions of the letter of intent, they shall apply to the University for final approval through the new College Accreditation System (NCPS) and the Universities shall submit the application of all eligible applicant institutions along with the compliance report to the Government through the new College Accreditation System (NCPS) along with the recommendations for approval.

4) If the management defaults in complying with the condition of the letter of intent within the time limit specified in clause (e) as specified in the proviso under section 109 (3) (f) of the Maharashtra Public University Act, 2016, the letter of intent shall be deemed to have lapsed.

3. Subject to the University taking the necessary further action regarding the grant of final recognition.
